mechanism_narrative: Declining payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 130515.0 thousand as of Feb 2011
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with fragile labor market
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or negative PAYEMS changes do not produce significantly lower 20-day forward returns than positive changes (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate near zero (FEDFUNDS at 0.16%) combined with sustained low rates signals monetary accommodation that reduces equity risk premiums and supports forward equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 55% of periods when FEDFUNDS < 0.50%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) combined with expanding housing starts (HOUST) indicates robust aggregate demand that supports corporate revenue growth and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 387123.0 million and HOUST at 596.0 thousand as of Jan 2011
regime_hint: early-cycle expansion with synchronized demand signals
failure_condition: Joint positive moves in RSAFS and HOUST do not produce significantly higher 20-day returns than single-series positive moves (interaction F-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC of interaction term < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production (INDPRO) signals supply-side weakness and reduced capacity utilization that constrains corporate profitability and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month INDPRO declines > 0.5% do not produce significantly negative 5-day returns compared to flat or positive INDPRO periods (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) combined with stagnant payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals persistent labor slack that dampens wage growth and consumer confidence, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 8.9% and PAYEMS at 130515.0 thousand as of Feb 2011
regime_hint: labor market deterioration in recovery phase
failure_condition: Simultaneous UNRATE increases and PAYEMS stagnation do not produce significantly lower 60-day returns than periods with improving labor metrics (t-test p > 0.05), or interaction Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 2.5% annual rate combined with accommodative FEDFUNDS signals real rate compression that erodes equity risk premiums and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: High inflation periods (YoY CPIAUCSL > 2.5%) with low FEDFUNDS do not produce significantly lower 20-day returns than low-inflation periods (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) declining below 600 thousand units annualized signals weakening residential investment demand that reduces construction employment and material demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 596.0 thousand as of Jan 2011
failure_condition: HOUST below 600 thousand does not produce significantly lower 60-day forward returns than HOUST above 600 thousand (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) expanding above 95.0 index level combined with rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals broad-based supply and labor capacity growth that supports corporate earnings expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 95.1452 and PAYEMS at 130515.0 thousand as of Feb 2011
failure_condition: Joint increases in INDPRO and PAYEMS do not produce significantly higher 20-day returns than single-series increases (interaction F-test p > 0.10), or interaction Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.
